examples.text:
روز چرا چو آید کاعمال من بپرسد
گرید ملک بصد درد از سوزهای هویم
examples.ref: c. 1520, Selim I of the Ottoman Empire, edited by Benedek Péri, The Persian Dīvān of Yavuz Sulṭān Selīm, Budapest, Hungary: Research Centre for the Humanities, Eötvös Loránd Research Network, →ISBN, page 226:
examples.english:
When the Day [of Judgment] when he inquires my deeds arrives, why
Does the angel weep from my laments' anguish with a hundredfold pain?
examples.roman:
rōz čirā čū āyad k-a'māl-i man bipursad
giryad malak ba-sad dard az sōzhā-yi hōyam
examples.translation:
When the Day [of Judgment] when he inquires my deeds arrives, why
Does the angel weep from my laments' anguish with a hundredfold pain?

glosses: Persian (the language of modern Iran, Afghanistan and Tajikistan, and widely spoken in Uzbekistan; also its historical forms).

etymology_text:
Etymology tree
Old Persian 𐎱𐎠𐎼𐎿 (p-a-r-s /⁠pārs⁠/)
Middle Persian pʾls (/⁠pārs⁠/)
Middle Persian -yk' (/⁠-īg⁠/)
Middle Persian pʾlsyk' (/⁠pārsīg⁠/)
Early New Persian پَارْسِی (pārsī)
▲
Arabic فَارِسِيّ (fārisiyy)influ.
Persian فارسی
Inherited from Early New Persian پَارْسِی (pārsī), influenced by Arabic فَارِسِيّ (fārisiyy). By surface analysis, فارس (fārs /fârs, “Fars”) + ـی (-ī /-i, suffix forming adjectives). Compare Old Armenian պարսիկ (parsik), Sanskrit पारसिक (pārasika).
